reports.title: brightest star known
reports.query:
MATCH (s:Star)
WHERE s.mag IS NOT NULL
RETURN s.name AS brightestStar, s.mag AS magnitude
//the smaller the mag the brighter the star
ORDER BY s.mag ASC
LIMIT 1

reports.title: amount of stars in constellations
reports.query:
MATCH (s:Star)-[:IN_CONSTELLATION]->(c:Constellation)
RETURN c.name AS constellation,
       count(*) AS stars
ORDER BY stars DESC
LIMIT 40

reports.title: spectral type distribution
reports.query:
MATCH (s:Star)
WHERE s.spect IS NOT NULL

//take 1st char of spect(denotes major spect class)
WITH substring(s.spect, 0, 1) AS spectralType, s
WITH [spectralType, s.name] AS path, 1 AS val
//each star gets 1 as val(1 item)-hierarchical
RETURN path, val
LIMIT 2000

reports.title: distance distribution(lightyear)of stars
reports.query:
MATCH (s:Star)
WHERE s.dist IS NOT NULL
  AND s.dist < 100000

WITH
CASE
    WHEN s.dist*3.262 < 50 THEN '0-50 ly'
    WHEN s.dist*3.262 < 100 THEN '50-100 ly'
    WHEN s.dist*3.262 < 500 THEN '100-500 ly'
    WHEN s.dist*3.262 < 1000 THEN '500-1000 ly'
    ELSE '1000+ ly'
END AS range

RETURN range, count(*) 
ORDER BY range

reports.title: distance vs luminosity
reports.query:
MATCH (s:Star)
WHERE s.dist IS NOT NULL
  AND s.lum IS NOT NULL

RETURN
round(s.dist * 3.262,1) AS DistanceLightYear,
round(s.lum,2) AS Luminosity

ORDER BY DistanceLightYear ASC
LIMIT 50

reports.title: stars in 1+ constellations
reports.query:
MATCH (s:Star)-[:IN_CONSTELLATION]->(c:Constellation)

WITH s, split(c.name, ";") AS parts

WHERE size(parts) > 1

RETURN
s.name AS Star,
//list comprehension
//for all x in parts,remove whitespace
[ x IN parts | trim(x) ] AS SharedConstellations,
s.mag AS Magnitude,
s.dist AS Distance
ORDER BY s.mag

reports.title: most similar star to selected one
reports.query:
MATCH (target:Star {name:$neodash_starname})
//ensure target has these numeric values for computing
WHERE target.mag IS NOT NULL
  AND target.dist IS NOT NULL
  AND target.lum IS NOT NULL

MATCH (s:Star)
//diff star and not same, non-null for computing
WHERE s.hip <> target.hip
  AND s.mag IS NOT NULL
  AND s.dist IS NOT NULL
  AND s.lum IS NOT NULL

WITH target,s,
//this measures how "far apart" 2 stars are according to these properties
//abs-the smaller the diff, the more similar so sort by asc
abs(s.mag - target.mag) +
abs(s.dist - target.dist)/1000 +
//dist and lum values can be very large so scale them down
//for them not to dominate/overshadow calculation
abs(s.lum - target.lum)/100000 AS similarityScore

RETURN
s.name AS SimilarStar,
round(similarityScore,2) AS Score,
s.mag AS Magnitude,
s.dist AS Distance,
s.lum AS Luminosity
ORDER BY Score DESC
LIMIT 10

reports.title: select a star
reports.query:
MATCH (s:Star)
WHERE s.name IS NOT NULL
RETURN DISTINCT s.name AS value
ORDER BY value

reports.title: About this page
reports.query:
This page provides a basic summary of all stars in the dataset.
It focuses on basic stellar properties such as magnitude, luminosity, distance, and spectral, which constellations they belong to.
You can also find the most similar star to the one you select.

reports.title: choose constellation
reports.query:
RETURN "Any" AS value
UNION
MATCH (c:Constellation)
RETURN c.name AS value
ORDER BY c.name

reports.title: choose distance range
reports.query:
MATCH (s:Star)
WHERE s.dist IS NOT NULL 
WITH DISTINCT 
//truncating with buckets
toInteger((s.dist*3.262) / 10) * 10 AS bucket 
RETURN bucket AS value
ORDER BY value

reports.title: stars in selected constellation and distance
reports.query:
MATCH (s:Star)-[:IN_CONSTELLATION]->(c:Constellation)
WHERE c.name = $neodash_constellation
  AND s.dist IS NOT NULL
  AND s.mag IS NOT NULL
  AND toFloat(s.dist) <= $neodash_maxdistance

RETURN
s.name AS Star,
s.mag AS Magnitude,
s.dist*(3.262) AS DistanceLightYears,//dist is in parsecs
s.spect AS SpectralType,
c.name AS Constellation
ORDER BY s.mag DESC
LIMIT 20

reports.title: spectral types in selected constellation
reports.query:
MATCH (s:Star)-[:IN_CONSTELLATION]->(c:Constellation)
WHERE c.name = $neodash_constellation
  AND s.spect IS NOT NULL

WITH substring(s.spect,0,1) AS spectralClass,
     count(*) AS stars

RETURN spectralClass, stars

reports.title: stars by magnitude var in const
reports.query:
MATCH (s:Star)-[:IN_CONSTELLATION]->(c:Constellation)
WHERE c.name = $neodash_constellation
  AND s.var_min IS NOT NULL
  AND s.var_max IS NOT NULL

RETURN
s.name AS Star,
s.var AS VariableType,
round(s.var_max - s.var_min,2) AS MagnitudeVariation
ORDER BY MagnitudeVariation DESC
LIMIT 15

reports.title: selected constellation network
reports.query:
MATCH (s:Star)-[i:IN_CONSTELLATION]->(c:Constellation)
WHERE c.name = $neodash_constellation
RETURN s,c,i
LIMIT 300

reports.title: unusually bright stars in selected constellation
reports.query:
MATCH (s:Star)-[:IN_CONSTELLATION]->(c:Constellation)
WHERE c.name = $neodash_constellation
  AND s.lum IS NOT NULL

WITH avg(s.lum) AS avgLum,//avg lum in const c
     stDev(s.lum) AS stdLum,//how spread out luminosities are
     c//WITH carries c into next match

MATCH (star:Star)-[:IN_CONSTELLATION]->(c)
WHERE star.lum > avgLum + 2*stdLum//basically ~95% brighter than other strs

RETURN
star.name AS Star,
round(star.lum,2) AS Luminosity,
round(avgLum,2) AS ConstellationAverage
ORDER BY star.lum DESC

settings.noDataMessage: no such star in this const

reports.title: highest spectral diversity constellations + value by rarity
reports.query:
MATCH (s:Star)-[:IN_CONSTELLATION]->(c:Constellation)
//rows
WITH c, substring(s.spect, 0, 1) AS type, count(*) AS typeCount//count of spect-type stars

WITH c,
     collect({type: type, count: typeCount}) AS data,//lists
     collect(type) AS types,
     sum(typeCount) AS total//total stars in const

UNWIND data AS d//turn back into rows

WITH c,
     d.count AS typeCount,
     total,
     types,
     (1.0 / d.count) AS rarity //more count/occurence of one type-larger num

WITH c,
     sum(rarity) AS raritySum,//sum rarities of spect in this const
     total,//total stars in const
     types

RETURN
c.name AS name,
types,
raritySum * total AS value //diversity,scaling by size
ORDER BY value DESC
LIMIT 20

reports.title: About this page
reports.query: This page shows information about selected constellation. Focuses on the star properties/diversities in the constellation. You can see the visual network of stars in the constellation.

reports.title: meteor future risk prediction map
reports.query:
MATCH (m:Meteorite)
WHERE m.location IS NOT NULL

WITH m,//normalize missing values
coalesce(m.mass, 1) AS mass,
coalesce(m.year, 2000) AS year

SET m.intensity =
log10(mass + 1) * (1 + (year / 2026.0))//log10 prevents huge meteorites from dominating the map,
//mass+1 if mass=0
//recent years get higher multiplier
//+1 for scaling since always<1

RETURN m
LIMIT 1000

reports.title: meteorites per year
reports.query:
MATCH (m:Meteorite)
WHERE m.year IS NOT NULL AND m.year>=1900
RETURN m.year AS Year, count(*) AS Count
ORDER BY Year
LIMIT 50

reports.title: heaviest meteorites
reports.query:
MATCH (m:Meteorite)
WHERE m.mass IS NOT NULL
RETURN m.name, m.mass
ORDER BY m.mass DESC
LIMIT 10

reports.title: meteorites fall location by mass and year
reports.query:
MATCH (m:Meteorite)
WHERE m.location IS NOT NULL
  AND (
    m.year IS NULL OR
  (toInteger(m.year) >= date($neodash_startyear).year
  )
AND (toInteger(m.year) <= date($neodash_endyear).year
  )
  AND (
    m.mass IS NULL OR
  (toFloat(m.mass) >= toFloat($neodash_minmass) )
AND   (toFloat(m.mass) <=toFloat($neodash_maxmass))
)
)
RETURN m

reports.title: select min mass
reports.query: RETURN true;

reports.title: select start date
reports.query: RETURN true;

reports.title: select end date
reports.query: RETURN true;

reports.title: select max mass
reports.query: RETURN true;

reports.title: About this page
reports.query:
This page focuses on recorded meteorite landings and their physical properties such as mass, location, and discovery year.
We also calculate the future risk of metorite landing spots.
You can see which meteors landed where by selecting time and mass properties.

reports.title: planet size hierarchy
reports.query:
MATCH (p:Planet)

OPTIONAL MATCH (p)-[:ORBITS]->(:Star)

WITH p,
CASE
  WHEN p.massEarth IS NULL THEN "Unknown"

  WHEN p.massEarth < 0.5 THEN "Sub-earth"
  WHEN p.massEarth < 2 THEN "Earth-like"
  WHEN p.massEarth < 10 THEN "Super-earth"
  WHEN p.massEarth < 30 THEN "Mini-neptune"
  WHEN p.massEarth < 100 THEN "Neptune-like"
  WHEN p.massEarth < 300 THEN "Saturn-like"
  ELSE "Gas giant"
END AS type

WITH
[
  type,
  substring(p.name, 0, 3)
] AS hierarchy,

coalesce(p.massEarth,1) AS mass

RETURN hierarchy, mass
LIMIT 1000

reports.title: planets orbiting selected star
reports.query:
MATCH (s:Star)
WHERE coalesce(s.name,s.star_name,toString(s.hip)) = $neodash_star_name

MATCH (p:Planet)-[o:ORBITS]->(s)

RETURN DISTINCT
p.name AS Planet,
p.massEarth AS MassEarth,
o.periodDays AS OrbitDays,
p.equilibriumTemp AS Temperature
ORDER BY p.massEarth DESC

reports.title: select a star
reports.query:
MATCH (s:Star)<-[:ORBITS]-(p:Planet)
WITH DISTINCT s
RETURN DISTINCT
coalesce(s.name, s.star_name, toString(s.hip)) AS value

reports.title: star systems
reports.query:
MATCH (p:Planet)-[r:ORBITS]->(s:Star)
return DISTINCT p, r, s 
LIMIT 1000

reports.title: earth-like planets
reports.query:
MATCH (p:Planet)-[:ORBITS]->(s:Star)

WHERE p.massEarth IS NOT NULL
  AND p.equilibriumTemp IS NOT NULL
  AND p.insolation IS NOT NULL

  AND (s.mass IS NULL OR
       (s.mass >= toFloat($neodash_minmass)
AND s.mass <= toFloat($neodash_maxmass)))

  AND (s.distance IS NULL OR
       s.distance <= toFloat($neodash_distance))

  AND (s.temperature IS NULL OR
       s.temperature <= toFloat($neodash_maxtemp))

WITH p, s,
     1.0 AS earthMass,
     288.0 AS earthTemp,
     1.0 AS earthInsolation

WITH p, s, earthMass, earthTemp, earthInsolation,

     1 / (1 + abs(p.massEarth - earthMass)) AS massScore,
     1 / (1 + abs(p.equilibriumTemp - earthTemp) / 50) AS tempScore,
     1 / (1 + abs(p.insolation - earthInsolation)) AS insolationScore

WITH p, s,
     (massScore * 0.4 +
      tempScore * 0.4 +
      insolationScore * 0.2) AS habitabilityRaw

RETURN DISTINCT
p.name AS Planet,
round(habitabilityRaw * 100, 1) AS HabitabilityIndex,
round(p.massEarth, 2) AS Mass,
round(p.equilibriumTemp, 0) AS TempK,
round(p.insolation, 2) AS Insolation,
coalesce(s.name, s.star_name, toString(s.hip)) AS HostStar

ORDER BY HabitabilityIndex DESC
LIMIT 50

reports.title: select min distance
reports.query:
MATCH (s:Star)
WHERE s.dist IS NOT NULL

RETURN
toInteger(s.dist) AS value

reports.title: select min mass
reports.query:
MATCH (p:Planet)
WHERE p.massEarth IS NOT NULL

RETURN
toInteger(p.massEarth) AS value

reports.title: select max mass
reports.query:
MATCH (p:Planet)
WHERE p.massEarth IS NOT NULL

RETURN
toInteger(p.massEarth) AS value

reports.title: select max temperature
reports.query:
MATCH (p:Planet)
WHERE p.equilibriumTemp IS NOT NULL

RETURN
toInteger(p.equilibriumTemp) AS value

reports.title: most habitable planets
reports.query:
MATCH (p:Planet)-[:ORBITS]->(s:Star)
WHERE p.equilibriumTemp IS NOT NULL

WITH p, s,
     abs(p.equilibriumTemp - 288.0) / 288.0 AS tempNorm,
//288K is earth's temp so whatever's close to that
     abs(coalesce(p.massEarth, 1) - 1.0) AS massDev,
     coalesce(p.eccentricity, 0) AS ecc//orbital eccentricity

WITH p, s,
(
//exp(-x), the closer to earth ->score near 1
  exp(-tempNorm) * 0.5 +//scaling
  exp(-massDev) * 0.3 +
  exp(-ecc) * 0.2
) AS habitabilityScore

RETURN DISTINCT
p.name AS Planet,
habitabilityScore,
round(p.equilibriumTemp,1) AS Temp,
coalesce(s.name, s.star_name) AS Star
ORDER BY habitabilityScore DESC
LIMIT 50

reports.title: general info about planets in selected star system and their discovery
reports.query:
MATCH (sys:System {name: $neodash_system_name})<-[:PART_OF]-(s:Star)
MATCH (s)<-[r:ORBITS]-(p:Planet)
MATCH (p)-[dr:DISCOVERED_BY]->(dm:DiscoveryMethod)
RETURN DISTINCT
  s.star_name AS star,
  p.name AS planet,
//create fake rel for visualizing
  apoc.create.vRelationship(
    p,
    'ORBITS (' + toString(coalesce(r.periodDays,0)) + 'd @ ' + toString(coalesce(round(r.semiMajorAxisAU*1000)/1000.0,0)) + 'AU)',
    properties(r),
    s
  ) AS orbitDisplay,
  dm.name AS discoveryMethod,
  apoc.create.vRelationship(
    p,
    'DISCOVERY_YEAR (' + toString(coalesce(dr.year,'?')) + ')',
    properties(dr),
    dm
  ) AS discoveryDisplay

reports.title: choose star system
reports.query:
MATCH (n:`System`) 
WHERE toLower(toString(n.`name`)) CONTAINS toLower($input) 
RETURN DISTINCT n.`name` as value,  n.`name` as display ORDER BY size(toString(value)) ASC LIMIT 5

reports.title: About this page
reports.query:
This page visualizes planetary systems and the exoplanets orbiting their host stars.
It focuses on planetary properties such as mass, radius, temperature, and orbital relationships. 
You can also find most habitable planets according to your input.

reports.title: score by diverse,spread out discovery facilities
reports.query:
MATCH (p:Planet)-[:DISCOVERED_AT]->(f:Facility)
MATCH (p)-[:ORBITS]->(s:Star)
WHERE 
  $neodash_facilityname = "Any"
  OR f.name = $neodash_facilityname


WITH f,
     count(p) AS planets,//how many planets discovered
     count(DISTINCT s) AS systems//how many systems searched
//distinct if multiple stars discovered in 1 system
//else would skew results
RETURN
f.name AS name,
(planets * log10(systems + 1)) AS score
ORDER BY score DESC

reports.title: planet discovery per facility
reports.query:
MATCH (p:Planet)-[:DISCOVERED_AT]->(f:Facility)

WHERE
  $neodash_facilityname = "Any"
  OR f.name = $neodash_facilityname // in case of Any, TRUE OR ...=TRUE,all rows returned

RETURN
f.name AS Facility,
count(p) AS PlanetsFound
ORDER BY PlanetsFound DESC
LIMIT 20

reports.title: discovery methods over time
reports.query:
MATCH (p:Planet)-[d:DISCOVERED_BY]->(m:DiscoveryMethod)
WHERE d.year IS NOT NULL

RETURN
d.year AS Year,
m.name AS Method,
count(*) AS Discoveries
ORDER BY Year, Method

reports.title: choose facility
reports.query:
MATCH (f:Facility)
WHERE f.name IS NOT NULL

WITH collect(DISTINCT f.name) AS Facilities
//add any to that list
UNWIND (["Any"] + Facilities) AS value
//rows +Any for dropdown
RETURN
value AS label,
value AS value
ORDER BY label

reports.title: which facility was the planet discovered at
reports.query:
MATCH (p:Planet)-[d:DISCOVERED_AT]->(f:Facility)

WHERE p.name = $neodash_planet_name

RETURN
f.name AS Facility

reports.title: choose planet
reports.query:
MATCH (n:`Planet`) 
WHERE toLower(toString(n.`name`)) CONTAINS toLower($input) 
RETURN DISTINCT n.`name` as value,  n.`name` as display ORDER BY size(toString(value)) ASC LIMIT 5

reports.title: discovery method analytics by facilities by years
reports.query:
WITH
range($neodash_discovered_by_year_1, $neodash_discovered_by_year_2) AS years,//list of years
$neodash_facility_name AS f1,
$neodash_facility_name_2 AS f2

UNWIND years AS y

MATCH (f:Facility)
WHERE f.name IN [f1, f2]

MATCH (p:Planet)-[:DISCOVERED_AT]->(f)
OPTIONAL MATCH (p)-[db:DISCOVERED_BY]->(dm:DiscoveryMethod)
WHERE toInteger(db.year) = y

WITH
f, y, p, dm,
coalesce(dm.name, "UNKNOWN") AS method

WITH f.name AS facility,
     y AS year,
     method,
     count(DISTINCT p) AS planet_count
//total planets per year per facility
CALL {
    WITH facility, year
    MATCH (f:Facility {name: facility})
    MATCH (p:Planet)-[:DISCOVERED_AT]->(f)
    MATCH (p)-[db:DISCOVERED_BY]->(:DiscoveryMethod)
    WHERE toInteger(db.year) = year
    RETURN count(DISTINCT p) AS total_planets
}

WITH facility, year, method, planet_count, total_planets,
CASE
  WHEN total_planets = 0 THEN 0.0
  ELSE 1.0 * planet_count / total_planets
END AS share//share of each method

WITH facility, year,
     collect({
        method: method,
        count: planet_count,
        share: share
     }) AS methods

WITH facility, year, methods,
     apoc.coll.sortMaps(methods, "count") AS sorted_methods

WITH facility, year, methods,
     head(reverse(sorted_methods)) AS top_method,
//reverse: largest->smallest, head->1st element aka best one
//how spread out are discoveries aka if one method dominates - low diversity
//reduce() is Cypher’s way of doing a loop over a list while accumulating a result
     reduce(s = 0.0, m IN methods |
        s + CASE
                WHEN m.share <= 0 THEN 0
                ELSE -1.0 * m.share * log(m.share)
            END
     ) AS diversity_score

RETURN
    facility,
    toString(year) AS year,
    top_method.method AS best_method,
    top_method.count AS PlanetsFoundByBestMethod,
    round(top_method.share * 100, 2) AS percentage_share,
    round(diversity_score, 4) AS diversity_index
ORDER BY diversity_index ASC;

WHERE toLower(toString(n.`year`)) CONTAINS toLower($input) 
RETURN DISTINCT n.`year` as value,  n.`year` as display ORDER BY size(toString(value)) ASC LIMIT 5

reports.title: planets by discovery type
reports.query:
MATCH (s:Star)<-[o:ORBITS]-(p:Planet)
OPTIONAL MATCH (p)-[d:DISCOVERED_BY]->(m:DiscoveryMethod)
  WHERE m IS NOT NULL

WITH s, p, o, d, m
WHERE m IS NOT NULL

RETURN DISTINCT s,p, o, d, m
LIMIT 45

reports.title: About this page
reports.query:
This page shows the analytics of observatories and discovery methods responsible for detecting planets. 
 You can also compare and see which facilities contribute most, what planet was discovered where, and what methods they used during each year, compare 2 facilities.
